current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Retry the same K=128 TopK Adam anchor after a narrow startup fix in `sparsify/__main__.py` that skips unnecessary rank-by-rank artifact loading when the model and dataset paths are local. full

Run a proxy `factorized_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether the added hidden mixing stage can beat the 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory on quality.

Run a proxy `mixture_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether soft expert routing can materially beat the current 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory.

action.hypothesis: The first-step timeout is caused by serialized DDP startup for local artifacts rather than by the K=128 TopK/Adam configuration itself; allowing both ranks to load the local model and dataset in parallel should get the proxy run to first metrics.
action.summary: Retry the same K=128 TopK Adam anchor after a narrow startup fix in `sparsify/__main__.py` that skips unnecessary rank-by-rank artifact loading when the model and dataset paths are local.
action.self_review: This targets startup latency only, so it may not help if the watchdog is being tripped by a different pre-step bottleneck such as dataset shuffling or hook initialization. The quality hypothesis itself remains untested until the run reaches metrics.
notes_to_memory: Round 1 crash still has no usable FVU signal; treat it as startup-path failure, not as evidence against TopK or Adam.
Local DDP artifact loading was serialized in sparsify/__main__.py; this round parallelizes local-path startup across ranks to reduce first-step latency.
next_hypotheses: If the retry reaches metrics, resume K=128 quality work before considering any lower-K point.
If startup still times out, inspect dataset shuffle/select ordering and other pre-step costs in the local artifact path.
If the run succeeds but Adam does not materially improve K=128 quality, escalate to a new encoder family rather than continuing local optimizer search.

action.hypothesis: The new K=128 TopK+Adam anchor at FVU 0.211975 is strong enough to stop local optimizer tinkering for now; a more expressive nonlinear encoder is the next plausible path toward a materially lower FVU, so `factorized_topk` should be tested at the same K=128 anchor settings.
action.summary: Run a proxy `factorized_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether the added hidden mixing stage can beat the 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory on quality.
action.self_review: This introduces a new architecture family, so the first proxy result may underperform or show extra compute cost. If it is only marginally better than TopK, it still may not justify promotion given the aggressive goal of roughly halving the current baseline FVU.
notes_to_memory: A trustworthy K=128 quality anchor now exists: full TopK+Adam at FVU 0.2119751125574112.
With the anchor established and still far from the aggressive target, bias subsequent rounds toward architecture exploration rather than more local TopK retuning.
next_hypotheses: If `factorized_topk` shows materially better proxy FVU at K=128, promote it and then tune its encoder width or related family-specific capacity.
If `factorized_topk` is neutral or worse, try another expressive encoder family such as `mixture_topk` rather than returning immediately to smaller-K search.
If the new family is quality-promising but noticeably slower, treat that as a performance-fix follow-up instead of an immediate architectural rejection.

action.hypothesis: `factorized_topk` missed badly at K=128, so the next plausible quality-focused family is a softly routed multi-expert encoder; `mixture_topk` may improve reconstruction by adding conditional encoder capacity without the hard routing bottlenecks of grouped variants.
action.summary: Run a proxy `mixture_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether soft expert routing can materially beat the current 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory.
action.self_review: This is another first-pass architecture probe, so it may still be far from competitive or may need family-specific tuning before it shows value. If it is only slightly better than `factorized_topk` but still far behind TopK, that is not enough to justify promotion.
notes_to_memory: `factorized_topk` prototype quality was poor at K=128 proxy (FVU 0.6381136849522591) despite normal runtime, so simple nonlinear hidden mixing does not currently look like a path to the aggressive target.
Keep architecture exploration focused on families with qualitatively different encoder behavior rather than continuing local retuning of the weak `factorized_topk` prototype.
next_hypotheses: If `mixture_topk` shows a clear proxy quality gain over plain TopK at K=128, promote it and then tune family-specific capacity.
If `mixture_topk` is also clearly worse than TopK, shift the next architecture probe toward a different sparse activation family such as `jumprelu` or a new routed/multi-branch design.
If `mixture_topk` is promising but slow, follow with a targeted performance pass instead of discarding the family immediately.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The new K=128 TopK+Adam anchor at FVU 0.211975 is strong enough to stop local optimizer tinkering for now; a more expressive nonlinear encoder is the next plausible path toward a materially lower FVU, so `factorized_topk` should be tested at the same K=128 anchor settings.
next_steps: If `factorized_topk` shows materially better proxy FVU at K=128, promote it and then tune its encoder width or related family-specific capacity.
If `factorized_topk` is neutral or worse, try another expressive encoder family such as `mixture_topk` rather than returning immediately to smaller-K search.
If the new family is quality-promising but noticeably slower, treat that as a performance-fix follow-up instead of an immediate architectural rejection.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: `factorized_topk` missed badly at K=128, so the next plausible quality-focused family is a softly routed multi-expert encoder; `mixture_topk` may improve reconstruction by adding conditional encoder capacity without the hard routing bottlenecks of grouped variants.
next_steps: If `mixture_topk` shows a clear proxy quality gain over plain TopK at K=128, promote it and then tune family-specific capacity.
If `mixture_topk` is also clearly worse than TopK, shift the next architecture probe toward a different sparse activation family such as `jumprelu` or a new routed/multi-branch design.
If `mixture_topk` is promising but slow, follow with a targeted performance pass instead of discarding the family immediately.

If `mixture_topk` is also clearly worse than TopK, shift the next architecture probe toward a different sparse activation family such as `jumprelu` or a new routed/multi-branch design.
If `mixture_topk` is promising but slow, follow with a targeted performance pass instead of discarding the family immediately.
